narrative: BTC 20-hour 15m window. Price opens at $111.6k, prints a bearish supply zone (rejection-style order block) at ~$110.2k during the London-NY overlap, then breaks structure decisively in the 12:00-13:30 UTC window down to $107.5k. A low-side liquidity sweep at 14:30 UTC fails to trigger sustained buying — price continues lower to $106.9k before a recovery bounce creates a bullish fair value gap at 18:00 UTC.

ob.chapter_note: Chapter 1 — bearish OB formed by upper-wick rejection at supply. Bot uses the LuxAlgo median-range refinement: zone is the upper half of the candle range, not the full body. Wick ratio ~1.6; displacement to follow is significant (-3% within 16 bars).

fvg.chapter_note: Chapter 2 — bullish 3-candle FVG formed during the recovery bounce off the session low. Gap between bar 77's high and bar 79's low. Bullish direction despite the broader bearish window — teaches that FVGs are local structures, not directionally bound to the macro trend. Potential IFVG once price closes back below — see Chapter 2 'IFVG flip' edge case.

bos.chapter_note: Chapter 3 — bearish BOS. Decisive close at $108,754 broke through the swing-low cluster around $109,400-109,800 that had held since bar 52. This is the structural confirmation that the OB at bar 49 was distributing supply, not just a wick rejection. The 4-swing-mode validator would see LH-HL-LH-HH (uptrend) → first close below the HL = bearish CHoCH; subsequent close below LL = BOS.

sweep.chapter_note: Chapter 4 — low-side liquidity sweep. Bar 65 punctured below $108,086 (wick low 107,873) then closed back above at 108,318 — classic sweep signature. Confirmation level 1 (pattern only, no climactic volume). Pedagogically important: this is a FAILED sweep — the reversal did not sustain. Price continued to $106,869 by bar 76. Teaches the HRLR vs LRLR distinction from Chapter 4 — this was likely a low-resistance liquidity run, not a respected level.

wyckoff_phase.label: Phase D markdown
wyckoff_phase.chapter_note: Chapter 5 — full window is the markdown phase of a larger distribution that completed pre-window. The bearish OB is the LPSY (Last Point of Supply); the sweep at bar 65 is a weak rally attempt that failed. The recovery bounce at the end of the window is the start of a possible new accumulation Phase A.

regime_hint.label: volatile_trend_to_chop
regime_hint.chapter_note: Chapter 7 — first half of the window is a quiet-trend regime (slow drift down); the impulse from bar 55-61 is a sharp volatility expansion (volatile trend). The post-sweep range from bar 65 to bar 75 is a transitional chop before the recovery. Operator should see ATR% spike around bar 55-61.

kill_zone.chapter_note: Chapter 9 — Master Fixture does NOT contain a clean kill-zone aligned OB. OB at 10:30 UTC is just outside the London open window (07:00-10:00 UTC). Recommend a separate small fixture for the Chapter 9 24h clock-ring widget. Cross-reference: the sweep at 14:30 UTC IS inside the NY AM kill zone (13:30-16:00 UTC) — this is a teachable subtlety.

notes: Generated by backend/diagnostics/curate_master_fixture.py + manual enrichment (BOS marker, chapter notes, lens coverage). The 4-primitive colocation is real but not tight-clustered: events span 8 hours. Chapters reference this fixture by lens; per-chapter annotations cite the bar_idx so the SVG renderer can position markers without re-deriving from timestamps.
